description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rugged, angular profile and a dark, earthy coloration. The surface is predominantly rough and matte, with visible natural pitting and subtle color variations ranging from deep gray to reddish-brown. One face of the stone features hand-written markings or catalog numbers, suggesting it may have been collected or studied for research or construction purposes. The edges are sharp and uneven, with some flat planes and others more jagged, indicating it was likely broken from a larger rock. The stone's mass and density are apparent from its solid, compact form, and it appears to be composed entirely of a hard, fine-grained rock such as basalt or granite. Its size and shape make it suitable as an obstacle or barrier in urban environments, such as a roadside, park, or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, angular stone with a dark gray to reddish-brown surface, featuring rough texture and hand-written markings on one face. Its solid, dense form and irregular shape make it suitable as an urban obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: Tall, narrow face with a slightly pointed top and rough, dark surface; subtle reddish hues are visible.
description_view_1: Wider, sloped side showing more pronounced surface texture and angular edges, with deep gray coloration.
description_view_2: Flat end with visible hand-written markings and catalog numbers, lighter in color and more geometric.
description_view_3: Broad, flat underside with a mix of brown and gray tones, showing a smoother but still matte finish.
attribute_location: urban roadside, landscaped park, or construction site
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: No regular pattern; natural mottling and subtle color banding, with visible hand-written markings on one face.
attribute_weathering: Rough and matte with minor pitting, indicating moderate natural weathering but no significant erosion or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; markings suggest human interaction or cataloging.
